text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
author: John Johnson

text: Truth can only be found in one place: the code.
author: Robert C. Martin

text: Any fool can write code a computer can understand. Good programmers write code humans can understand.
author: Martin Fowler

text: Experience is the name everyone gives to their mistakes.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: Sometimes it pays to stay in bed on Monday, rather than spending the rest of the week debugging Monday's code.
author: Dan Salomon

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

text: Don't watch the clock; do what it does. Keep going.
author: Sam Levenson

text: Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out.
author: Robert Collier

text: The expert in anything was once a beginner.
author: Helen Hayes

text: You don't have to be great to start, but you have to start to be great.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: Study while others are sleeping; work while others are loafing.
author: William A. Ward

text: A little progress each day adds up to big results.
author: Satya Nani

text: Push yourself, because no one else is going to do it for you.
author: Unknown

text: Procrastination makes easy things hard, and hard things harder.
author: Mason Cooley

text: The future depends on what you do today.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Coffee: because adulting and studying is hard.
author: Unknown
